subject: Cloud Computing
title: Oracle Cloud's Late Bet
description: A quick, practical guide to Oracle Cloud's Late Bet.

hook.text: Oracle mocked cloud computing for years. Then it scrambled to build its own cloud — a decade late.

buildup.text: Larry Ellison dismissed cloud as 'complete gibberish' in 2008. By 2016, Oracle was racing to catch up.

discovery.text: Oracle Cloud targeted its massive existing database customer base: migrate your Oracle DB to our cloud.

twist.text: Oracle scored a surprise win: TikTok chose Oracle Cloud for U.S. data, boosting its credibility overnight.

climax.text: Oracle Cloud remains a niche player. But in databases, it competes fiercely with services no one else offers.

punchline.text: Oracle arrived late. Its database customers gave it a foothold.

quiz.question: How did Oracle Cloud gain initial traction despite launching late?
options: By targeting its existing database customers with migration incentives
By offering the cheapest cloud services in every category
By buying AWS and rebranding its services
